title: Columbus Partners with The Landfill Group for RNG Facility at Pinegrove Landfill
content: The Columbus Consolidated Government has announced a partnership with The Landfill Group to construct a Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) facility at the Pinegrove MSW Landfill. Construction, which began in 2025 after securing all necessary permits, is on track to be completed by the spring of 2026. Once fully operational, the facility will capture extracted methane and process it into a marketable renewable energy source, supporting both environmental sustainability and responsible landfill management. To celebrate this collaboration and Arbor Day in Georgia, the partners will host an invitation-only tree-planting ceremony and guided tours on February 20, 2026. A public ribbon-cutting ceremony will follow once commercial operations commence. This project highlights the city's commitment to converting waste into cleaner fuel.
author: Analysts Account
